notes: Forms in starchy foods cooked above 120C (fried, roasted, baked). IARC Group 2A: probably carcinogenic to humans.
Mold toxin produced by Aspergillus on improperly stored peanuts, corn, tree nuts. IARC Group 1: carcinogenic to humans.
Artificial sweetener. IARC Group 2B: possibly carcinogenic to humans (2023 classification).
Can form in beverages containing benzoate preservatives + ascorbic acid. IARC Group 1.
Ammonia process caramel coloring; can contain 4-methylimidazole. IARC Group 2B.
Sulfite-ammonia process caramel coloring; can contain 4-methylimidazole. IARC Group 2B.
Forms naturally in fermented foods and alcoholic beverages. IARC Group 2A.
Used to sterilize spices and dried foods. Residues are a concern. IARC Group 1.
Forms in heat-processed foods (canned, jarred). IARC Group 2B.
Form when nitrites react with amines in cured meats during cooking. IARC Group 2A (most members).
Mold toxin found in stored grains, coffee, wine, dried fruits. IARC Group 2B.
Industrial chemicals that bioaccumulate in fatty fish. Banned in production but persist. IARC Group 1.
WHO classifies processed meat as Group 1 carcinogen. Includes any meat preserved by smoking, curing, salting, or chemical preservatives.
Unprocessed mammalian muscle meat. IARC Group 2A: probably carcinogenic to humans.
Forms during refining of vegetable oils. IARC Group 2B.
Forms during high-temperature refining of vegetable oils. IARC Group 2A.
Byproduct of certain caramel colorings (Class III, IV). IARC Group 2B.
Synthetic antioxidant used as preservative in fats. IARC Group 2B.
Artificial sweetener. IARC Group 3: not classifiable as carcinogenic to humans (downgraded from 2B in 1999).
Used in cured meats. Group 2A in the context of nitrate/nitrite ingestion under conditions resulting in endogenous nitrosation.
Used in cured meats; converts to nitrite. IARC Group 2A under conditions resulting in endogenous nitrosation.
Flour improver. Banned in EU, UK, Canada, India. Still allowed in some countries. IARC Group 2B.
White colorant. Banned in EU foods since 2022 over genotoxicity concerns. IARC Group 2B (inhaled).
Polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon. Forms in smoked, grilled, charred foods. IARC Group 1.
Some preservatives metabolize to formaldehyde. IARC Group 1.
Inorganic arsenic is a Group 1 carcinogen. Concentrates in rice grown in contaminated water.
Heavy metal. IARC Group 1. Accumulates in leafy vegetables, shellfish, organ meats, cocoa.
Heavy metal. IARC Group 2B (inorganic lead). Found in some spices, traditional medicines.
Methylmercury bioaccumulates in large fish (tuna, swordfish). IARC Group 2B.
Mold toxin found in apples and apple products. IARC Group 3 (not classifiable).
